betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Welcome to OldSchoolCraft! This quest book will guide you through the major mods and mechanics.

This is a hardcore survival experience with realistic torch burnout, food spoiling, temperature, and pitch-black nights. Read the §6Survival & Exploration§f chapter for tips!

Open the quest book anytime with L (default key).
It's that time again - punch some trees! You'll need plenty of wood. Vanilla tools are disabled by §9Iguana's Tinker Tweaks§f, so Tinker's Construct is your path forward.

As you may have discovered, vanilla pickaxes are disabled. Set up your Tinker's Construct workstation to craft tools.

You'll need: Crafting Station, Stencil Table, Part Builder, Tool Station, Tool Forge, and a few patterns.
Your first pickaxe, assembled in the Tool Station. §9Iguana's Tinker Tweaks§f modifies the harvest level of ores and pickaxes. This pickaxe will let you gather copper ore for the next upgrade.
The first cast upgrade. You'll need to make this in the smeltery. You can swap the head of your pickaxe in the Tool Station.

This pack includes §9Hardcore Darkness§f and §9Realistic Torches§f. Nights are pitch black and torches burn out over time!

Craft a furnace, make some charcoal, and light up your surroundings. Remember: torches are temporary, so plan for permanent lighting later.
A bit better than copper. Smelt iron in the smeltery and cast a pickaxe head.
The first alloy pickaxe head. Make bronze in the smeltery by combining copper and tin at a 3:1 ratio.
Check out §9Thermal Expansion§f or §9IndustrialCraft 2§f to make Refined Iron (Steel). This opens up obsidian-tier mining.

The smeltery is your first ore-doubling method. You'll need this to make metal tools.

The required amounts will suffice for a 3x3 inside smeltery, but you can build it up to 7x7 inside.
Check out §9Thaumcraft§f and make a Thaumium Pickaxe. This opens a new upgrade path through magic!
Next up, the Nether! You'll need obsidian-tier mining to get there.
Ardite and Cobalt can be found in the Nether. Mine ardite first - it's easier to find.
The highest-tier Tinker's Construct pickaxe head. Cobalt is rare but powerful. You can also alloy Ardite + Cobalt in the smeltery to make Manyullyn!

§9Thaumcraft§f is a magic mod about understanding and harnessing the mystical energies of the world.

Begin by finding a §6Silverwood§f or §2Greatwood§f tree and breaking it for special logs. You'll need these to craft your first wand.

§cNote:§f Taint biome spread has been disabled in this pack, but warp mechanics are still active - be cautious with forbidden research!

Right-click a bookshelf with your wand to create the §5Thaumonomicon§f - your complete guide to all things Thaumic.

Read it thoroughly! Every recipe, mechanic, and research topic is documented within.

Combine a §9Table§f, §9Scribing Tools§f (ink sac + glass bottle), and your §9Thaumonomicon§f on the table to set up a Research Table.

Research aspects by combining known aspects to discover new ones. Complete research notes to unlock new recipes.

You'll need plenty of ink sacs - consider setting up a squid farm!

These goggles let you see §baura nodes§f, vis levels, and essentia in the world. Essential for any aspiring thaumaturge.

Craft them after researching the appropriate topic in your Thaumonomicon.

Smelt iron with §5Praecantatio§f aspects in a Crucible to create §9Thaumium Ingots§f - a versatile magical metal used in countless recipes.

You'll need a good supply of these as you progress through Thaumcraft.

Build the §5Infusion Altar§f to create powerful enchanted items.

The altar requires a §9Runic Matrix§f in the center surrounded by §9Arcane Pedestals§f. Stability is critical - add symmetrical items, candles, and skulls nearby to reduce instability.

Unstable infusions can have... unpredictable results.

OldSchoolCraft includes several Thaumcraft addons:

§9Forbidden Magic§f - Dark arts and cross-mod integration
§9Thaumic Tinkerer§f - Useful baubles, enchants, and the Kami tier
§9Thaumic Exploration§f - New wand foci and research
§9Thaumic Horizons§f - Pocket dimensions and golem upgrades
§9Witching Gadgets§f - Tools, baubles, and Thaumcraft-IE integration
§9Thaumic Energistics§f - Connect Thaumcraft to your AE2 network!

§4Blood Magic§f is a dark art that draws power from life force. Your §cLP§f (Life Points) fuel everything - from crafting slates to powering rituals.

The path is painful but rewarding. Start by crafting a Sacrificial Knife and building a Blood Altar.

The first step on the path of blood. Use this on yourself (right-click) while standing on a Blood Altar to fill it with LP.

It hurts... but power has a price. Each use costs half a heart of health.

The Blood Altar is where all Blood Magic crafting happens. Place it down and use your Sacrificial Knife while standing on it to fill it with LP.

To craft items, place them on the altar when it has enough LP stored.

Place §7Smooth Stone§f in a filled Blood Altar (1000 LP) to create Blank Slates - the foundation of all Blood Magic crafting.

You'll need many of these, so keep that altar topped up!

Upgrade your altar to §cTier 2§f by surrounding it with Blood Runes, then craft Reinforced Slates from Blank Slates.

Each altar tier unlocks more powerful recipes and requires specific rune arrangements.

Master the art of rituals with the §4Ritual Diviner§f and §4Master Ritual Stones§f.

Rituals can do incredible things: create water, magnetize items, accelerate growth, and much more.

The §9Blood Arsenal§f addon adds even more tools and features to your crimson arsenal. Bound tools and armor await those who push deeper!

§2Botania§f is a tech mod disguised as a magic mod. It uses mana generated from flowers to power devices and craft magical materials.

No pipes, no wires - just nature and ingenuity. Start by finding Mystical Flowers in the world!

Craft the §2Lexica Botania§f by combining a Book with any Mystical Petal in a crafting grid.

This is your complete guide to Botania - read everything!

The Petal Apothecary is your first functional flower crafting station. Fill it with water, then toss in petals to create magical flowers.

Start with a §ePure Daisy§f - it converts wood and stone into Livingwood and Livingrock, the basic Botania materials.

The Mana Pool stores mana generated by your flowers. Place a §bMana Spreader§f between your generating flowers and the pool to transfer mana.

Daybloom and Nightshade are your earliest mana generators - one works by day, the other by night.

Throw an §7Iron Ingot§f into a Mana Pool with sufficient mana to create §bManasteel§f.

This versatile magical metal is used in countless Botania recipes. You can also create §bMana Diamonds§f and §bMana Pearls§f the same way.

The ultimate Botania metal. Create it using a §2Terrestrial Agglomeration Plate§f on top of a Mana Pool.

Place a Manasteel Ingot, a Mana Diamond, and a Mana Pearl on the plate, then supply massive amounts of mana. Check your Lexica for the multiblock layout!

OldSchoolCraft features several tech mods working in harmony.

§6Thermal Expansion§f and §aEnderIO§f use RF (Redstone Flux).
§eIndustrialCraft 2§f uses EU (Energy Units).
§9Immersive Engineering§f adds realistic multiblock machines.
§8Railcraft§f provides rail systems and steel production.

Each has unique strengths - mix and match!

The §6Pulverizer§f grinds ores into dust, effectively doubling your ore output. It also processes many other materials.

Powered by Redstone Flux (RF). Pair with a Dynamo or any RF generator.

The §6Induction Smelter§f combines two inputs with heat to create alloys and process ores. Essential for creating Hardened Glass, machine frames, and many other recipes.

Tip: Smelting ore with Rich Slag gives a chance for extra ingots!

§aEnderIO§f's SAG Mill grinds ores and items with a chance for bonus outputs. Pair it with §aGrinding Balls§f for significantly better yields.

Dark Steel grinding balls are some of the best early game options.

The §aAlloy Smelter§f creates EnderIO's signature alloys:

§7Electrical Steel§f - Base machine material
§6Energetic Alloy§f - Energy storage
§aVibrant Alloy§f - Advanced tech
§4Soularium§f - Soul-powered devices
§8Dark Steel§f - Powerful tools and armor

Essential for EnderIO machines and conduits.

§eIC2§f uses EU (Energy Units) - a separate power system from RF.

Start with a §eGenerator§f, then build a §eMacerator§f (ore doubling) and §eElectric Furnace§f.

§cWarning:§f IC2 machines explode if given too much voltage! Use transformers to step down power.

IC2 also provides the Refined Iron needed for Steel pickaxe heads.

§9Immersive Engineering§f adds beautiful multiblock machines with a realistic industrial aesthetic.

Start by crafting an §9Engineer's Manual§f (book + lever). Then build a §9Coke Oven§f for Coal Coke and Creosote Oil.

Next, build a §9Blast Furnace§f for Steel, and a §9Crusher§f for ore doubling.

§8Railcraft§f provides advanced rail systems, the Coke Oven, and the Blast Furnace for steel production.

Railcraft's Coke Oven produces Coal Coke (better fuel) and Creosote Oil (used for Treated Wood in IE). Its Blast Furnace converts Iron to Steel.

Also adds powerful minecart systems for item and fluid transport.

§1Applied Energistics 2§f is the ultimate storage and autocrafting mod. It digitizes your items into a network of ME devices.

§eImportant:§f AE2 Meteorites (containing Inscriber Presses) spawn in the §9Mining Dimension§f - look for them there!

This mod is mid-to-late game and requires significant resources.

§bCertus Quartz§f is the foundation of AE2. Mine it underground.

Grow §bPure Certus Quartz Crystals§f by crafting Crystal Seeds and tossing them in water - they grow over time.

You'll also need §9Fluix Crystals§f - made by tossing Charged Certus Quartz, Nether Quartz, and Redstone together in water.

You'll need four §1Inscriber Presses§f from AE2 meteorites to create processor circuits. Found in the center of meteorites in the §9Mining Dimension§f.

The four presses: Calculation, Engineering, Logic, and Silicon.

Build an Inscriber to start crafting processors. Presses are reusable!

Build your ME network:

1. §1ME Controller§f - The brain
2. §1ME Drive§f - Holds storage cells
3. §1Storage Cells§f (1k/4k/16k/64k)
4. §1Crafting Terminal§f - Access your items
5. §1ME Cable§f - Connect everything

Don't forget power! An Energy Acceptor converts RF/EU for the network.

The crown jewel of AE2: automatic crafting!

Set up a §1Crafting CPU§f with Crafting Storage blocks. Encode patterns in the §1ME Pattern Terminal§f and insert them into §1Molecular Assemblers§f.

With Wireless Crafting Terminal (included in this pack!), you can request crafts from anywhere.

§9Extra Cells§f and §9AE2 Stuff§f add fluid storage and advanced inscribers.

OldSchoolCraft is not your typical modpack:

§cEnviroMine§f - Temperature, hydration, and sanity
§8Hardcore Darkness§f - Pitch black nights
§6Realistic Torches§f - Torches burn out
§aSpice of Life§f - Eat varied foods for full nourishment
§9Food Spoiling§f - Food expires over time
§4Infernal Mobs§f - Some mobs have special powers

Stay warm, stay hydrated, eat well, and always carry extra torches!

Torches burn out after some time thanks to §6Realistic Torches§f.

§ePermanent light sources:§f
- Glowstone blocks/lamps
- Jack-o-Lanterns
- Redstone Lamps
- Magnum Torches (Extra Utilities)
- EnderIO illumination

§cNote:§f Stone torches from Tinker's Construct have been disabled to maintain this balance.

Food spoils over time! Here's the tier system:

§c10 days§f - Raw meat, fish, eggs, milk
§614 days§f - Fruits, vegetables, mushrooms
§e21 days§f - Cooked meat, stews (default)
§a28 days§f - Bread, cookies, baked goods
§bNever spoils§f - Preserved foods!

Preserved foods include: §fJerky, pickles, salt-cured items, honey-preserved items, fruit jellies, and vinegar-pickled foods§f from HarvestCraft.

The world is full of dangerous dungeons!

§4Roguelike Dungeons§f - Massive underground complexes with floors of increasing difficulty.

§9Runic Dungeons§f - Mystical underground chambers.

§5Recurrent Complex§f - Ruins, structures, and points of interest.

§cInfernal Mobs§f makes some monsters extra deadly with random enchantments!

§dWaystones§f provide fast travel between discovered locations.

Right-click a Waystone to activate it, then use any other Waystone to teleport. Look for them in villages!

You can also craft your own Waystones for your bases.

§eMillenaire§f adds living NPC villages that grow and develop over time. Each culture (Norman, Hindu, Japanese, Mayan, Byzantine, Inuit) has unique buildings and goods.

Trade with villagers, help them gather resources, and watch their civilization expand.

You can even become the leader of a village!

Useful things to know:

§bNature's Compass§f - Locate specific biomes
§6Ore Excavation§f - Mine veins quickly
§aStorage Drawers + JABBA§f - Bulk storage
§9OpenComputers§f - Program Lua computers
§dIron Backpacks§f - Portable storage
§8BiblioCraft§f - Decorative shelves
§eCarpenter's Blocks§f - Slopes and stairs
§fChisel + Ztones§f - Decorative blocks
§6Better Builders Wands§f - Fast building!

Mine obsidian and build a portal to the Nether. A whole new dimension of resources and danger awaits!

You'll need Nether materials for many mod progressions: Blaze rods, Nether Quartz, Glowstone, and rare ores like Ardite and Cobalt.

§9Nether Ores§f mod adds all overworld ores to the Nether - but mining them can cause explosions!

Craft Eyes of Ender and find the Stronghold. The End dimension awaits beyond the portal.

You'll need Blaze Powder and Ender Pearls. Throw Eyes of Ender into the air and follow their direction to find the Stronghold.

The ultimate survival challenge. Collect Wither Skeleton skulls from the Nether Fortress and summon the Wither.

Defeat it to claim the §eNether Star§f - used for Beacons and many mod recipes. You'll need several of these eventually!

Journey to The End and slay the Ender Dragon. Claim the §5Dragon Egg§f as proof of your victory!

Thanks to §9Et Futurum§f, you can respawn the dragon just like in modern Minecraft.
